arts.ttl: Inter in Udine to go again after the derby
arts.sum: Inter have won six of their last seven matches against Udinese in Serie A (L1), scoring 19 goals in this period (2.7 per game); the Friulians` only success in this time came back in September 2022 (3-1 at the Blueenergy Stadium). After their 0-3 defeat against Roma, Udinese ended a nine-match unbeaten run in Serie A (W5 D4); the Friulians have not recorded two defeats in a row in Serie A since April (three, the first of which was against Inter).

arts.ttl: Inter’s defensive dilemma: Acerbi or De Vrij – Only one contract extension on the horizon
arts.sum: Inter Milan finds itself at a crossroads regarding the futures of two key defenders, Francesco Acerbi and Stefan De Vrij. With both players’ contracts set to expire on June 30, 2025, and the club’s new ownership, OakTree, looking to rejuvenate the squad, it appears that only one of these experienced center-backs will receive a contract extension. Tuttosport report that Acerbi, who will turn 37 next June, currently holds the starting position and has the advantage of being Italian, a factor that has historically been important to Inter’s management, particularly President Marotta. arts.ttl: Runjaic on Inter match: “We must perform in front of our fans”
arts.sum: Udinese head coach Kosta Runjaic addressed the media on Thursday ahead of his team’s upcoming Serie A clash against Inter Milan. The coach provided injury updates and shared his thoughts on the team’s preparation for the challenging fixture. Runjaic began by discussing the injury situation, confirming that Martin Payero will miss the Inter game due to a swollen foot. The extent of the injury is still unknown, with diagnostic tests scheduled for next week. The coach expressed hope that Payero might return after the international break. arts.ttl: Inter’s new stadium plans: Rozzano landowners provide update
arts.sum: The owners of the land in Rozzano, where Inter Milan are considering building their new stadium, have provided an update on the ongoing feasibility studies. The Brioschi-Cabassi group, who own the property, revealed details about the exclusive rights agreement with Inter and potential alternative plans. In their financial report, the real estate company disclosed that Inter’s exclusive rights to evaluate the Rozzano site have been extended. “On July 28, 2023, Infrafin granted FC Internazionale Milano S.p.A. a paid exclusive right until April 30, 2024, to verify the possibility of building a stadium and some accessory functions within the property area The post Inter’s new stadium plans: Rozzano landowners provide update appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Roma targeting Inter executive in Friedkin’s restructuring plan
arts.sum: The Friedkin family’s ongoing revolution at Roma continues unabated following the recent upheaval caused by Daniele De Rossi’s departure and Lina Souloukou’s subsequent resignation. While the search for a new CEO is still underway, with Lorenzo Vitali temporarily promoted to the role amid internal disagreements, the club’s ownership is already looking ahead to further strengthen its management structure. Dan and Ryan Friedkin, who have recently completed the acquisition of Everton in England, are not content with just addressing the CEO position. arts.ttl: Chelsea dodged a €100m Lautaro Martinez bullet
arts.sum: However, Barcelona youngster Marc Guiu was their only number nine addition during the summer window, and the 18-year-old has played an extremely limited role so far this season. He already has four goals and two assists in just five Premier League appearances this season, and his form has left Christopher Nkunku as an expensive back-up.
